context_state.divergence_note: Production hosting decision changed: Oracle Ampere A1 unavailable (free tier inventory exhausted across regions). Hetzner Cloud CX-series (Ashburn, VA) selected instead. Server provisioned and running. Node.js + npm dependencies installed. Model files missing — only calibration.json present via git. This command-request uses the new command-request/v1 protocol extension (docs/command-request-v1-spec.md).

payload.subject: Hetzner pivot + model file transfer request
payload.summary: Oracle A1 free tier unavailable. Hetzner CX (Ashburn, $5/mo, Debian 13, 4 GB RAM) provisioned as PSQ production host. Server running at 178.156.229.103. Node.js 20, npm deps installed. Model files (gitignored) needed from Chromabook. New protocol: docs/command-request-v1-spec.md + docs/bft-design-note.md.
payload.semantic_rename_note: transport/sessions/ directories renamed this session: subagent-protocol and psychology-interface now use semantic names. All files renamed to semantic names. session_id values updated in our files. Your files retain old session_id (historical record). Future messages: use psychology-agent/psychology-interface.

command.description: Transfer PSQ model files from Chromabook to Hetzner production server. Model files are gitignored and exist only on the Chromabook. The Hetzner server has calibration.json (from git) but lacks model.onnx, model_quantized.onnx, config.json, and tokenizer/.
preconditions: Model files present at ~/projects/psychology/safety-quotient/models/psq-student/ (model.onnx, config.json, tokenizer/)
SSH access to root@178.156.229.103 (Hetzner server — SSH key already authorized)
Network connectivity from Chromabook to Hetzner Ashburn

expected_outcome.description: All model files present at /opt/psychology-agent/safety-quotient/models/psq-student/ on Hetzner server
expected_outcome.verification_method: sha256sum of model.onnx on Hetzner matches Chromabook source; ls -la shows all required files

authorization.authorization_context: Session 22 — user approved Hetzner deployment and model transfer

post_transfer_steps: 2. Update systemd service User from 'ubuntu' to 'psq': edit /opt/psychology-agent/safety-quotient/deploy/psq-server.service
3. systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable psq-server && systemctl start psq-server
4. Verify: curl http://localhost:3000/health → {status: ok, model_ready: true}
5. Reply with command-response including state_attestation (file hashes + health check)

claims.text: Hetzner server (178.156.229.103) is running Debian 13, Node.js v20.20.1, with npm dependencies installed in /opt/psychology-agent/safety-quotient/
SSH key kashif@internal.kashifshah.net is authorized on the Hetzner server for root access
The psq-server.service systemd unit references User=ubuntu which needs changing to User=psq on Hetzner

epistemic_flags: Chromabook SSH key not yet tested against Hetzner — psq-agent may need user to add its public key
Model file paths on Chromabook assumed to match ~/projects/psychology/safety-quotient/models/psq-student/ — verify before executing
psq-server.service designed for Oracle Ubuntu — may need additional adaptation beyond User= change for Debian 13 (e.g., node path, working directory)

action_gate.gate_condition: psq-agent executes rsync + service setup and replies with command-response including state_attestation
action_gate.gate_note: First use of command-request/v1 protocol. See docs/command-request-v1-spec.md for response schema. After successful transfer: macOS runs wrangler secret put PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL → PSQ production live end-to-end.
